subject: science
title: Grade 5 Science
description: Earth and space, human body systems, and properties of materials.

lessons.title: Earth and Space
lessons.objective: Understand the Solar System, day/night, and seasons.
lessons.content:
Our SOLAR SYSTEM has the Sun at the centre with 8 planets orbiting it.

☀️ The Sun is a STAR — a giant ball of hot gas that gives us light and heat.

Planets in order from the Sun:
  Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
  (My Very Excited Mother Just Served Us Nachos!)

🌍 DAY and NIGHT happen because Earth ROTATES (spins) on its axis.
  One full rotation = 24 hours (1 day)
  The side facing the Sun has day; the other side has night.

🌸 SEASONS happen because Earth is TILTED on its axis.
  When our part tilts toward the Sun → Summer (more sunlight)
  When our part tilts away from the Sun → Winter (less sunlight)
  Earth takes 365.25 days to orbit the Sun (1 year).

exercises.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
exercises.hint: Earth spins like a top — one side faces the Sun at a time.
exercises.explanation: Earth rotates (spins) on its axis once every 24 hours. The side facing the Sun experiences day, and the other side has night.

exercises.question: How many planets are in our Solar System? (number only)
exercises.hint: Count: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune.
exercises.explanation: There are 8 planets in our Solar System: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

lessons.title: Human Body Systems
lessons.objective: Learn about the circulatory, respiratory, and digestive systems.
lessons.content:
Your body has amazing SYSTEMS that work together:

❤️ CIRCULATORY SYSTEM (blood circulation)
  • Heart pumps blood around the body
  • Blood carries oxygen and nutrients to cells
  • Arteries carry blood AWAY from heart, veins bring it BACK

🫁 RESPIRATORY SYSTEM (breathing)
  • Lungs take in oxygen from the air
  • We breathe out carbon dioxide (waste gas)
  • Air goes: nose/mouth → windpipe → lungs

🍕 DIGESTIVE SYSTEM (breaking down food)
  • Mouth: chew and mix with saliva
  • Stomach: acids break food down further
  • Small intestine: nutrients absorbed into blood
  • Large intestine: water absorbed, waste formed

exercises.question: What organ pumps blood around the body?
exercises.hint: Put your hand on your chest — can you feel it beating?
exercises.explanation: The heart is a muscle that pumps blood around your body. It beats about 100,000 times a day!

exercises.question: Where are nutrients from food absorbed into the blood?
exercises.hint: Despite its name, it's very long — about 6 metres!
exercises.explanation: The small intestine absorbs nutrients from digested food into the bloodstream. It's about 6 metres long and lined with tiny finger-like villi.
